title: Agent Kit Project Documentation
subtitle: Shared setup toolkit for Claude Code commands, MCP servers, skills, hooks, and assistant docs.
audience: Developers and AI assistants maintaining Agent Kit or using it to bootstrap target projects.
summary: Agent Kit centralizes reusable Claude Code setup assets. Install once globally, then use project commands to initialize assistant guidance, install skills, and apply custom workflows consistently across repositories.

sections.title: What Agent Kit Provides
sections.intro: A single repository for reusable AI-agent setup assets and the scripts that install them.

items.title: Consistent project bootstrap
items.body: `/ak:init-project` prepares target repositories with assistant docs, settings guidance, and GitNexus-aware setup.

items.title: Reusable AI capabilities
items.body: `/ak:setup-skills` and `/ak:setup-custom` install curated skills, commands, and hooks without copying assets manually.

items.title: Safer automation
items.body: Custom hooks block dangerous shell actions, catch hardcoded secrets, and refresh GitNexus after edits.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.intro: Install Agent Kit once, then run the setup commands inside each target repository.

items.title: Clone and enter Agent Kit
items.body: Clone this repository and run commands from the repository root.

items.title: Install global assets
items.body: Run `python3 scripts/install.py` to copy global commands and register MCP permissions.

items.title: Initialize submodules
items.body: Run `python3 scripts/install.py --init-submodule` or `git submodule update --init --recursive` to prepare skill sources.

items.title: Check install status
items.body: Run `python3 scripts/install.py --check` to verify command and MCP setup.

items.title: Bootstrap a target project
items.body: In another project, run `/ak:init-project`, then `/ak:setup-skills` and `/ak:setup-custom` as needed.

blocks.content:
git clone <this-repo>
cd agent-kit
python3 scripts/install.py
python3 scripts/install.py --init-submodule
python3 scripts/install.py --check

sections.title: How the Pieces Fit Together
sections.intro: Agent Kit separates source assets from installed assets so updates remain traceable.

items.title: Source
items.body: Maintain commands in `global/`, reusable private assets in `custom/`, and project-maintenance assets in `.claude/`.

items.title: Install
items.body: `scripts/install.py` installs global commands and MCP permissions into the user's Claude configuration.

items.title: Bootstrap
items.body: Target projects use `/ak:init-project` to generate assistant-facing guidance and settings.

items.title: Extend
items.body: Optional skills, commands, and hooks are installed through `/ak:setup-skills` and `/ak:setup-custom`.

rows: Canonical source for globally installed commands and MCP definitions.
Personal or team assets installed selectively into target projects.
External skill libraries; do not edit directly.
Maintenance commands and skills for this repository only.
JSON source plus generated HTML documentation.
Installer, project initializer, and validation suites.

sections.title: Commands, MCP Servers, and Hooks
sections.intro: The main operational surface of Agent Kit: slash commands, MCP servers, custom commands, and safety hooks.

rows: Per-project setup wizard for assistant docs, hooks, MCP permissions, and GitNexus guidance.
Install selected commands, skills, and hooks from `custom/`.
Install selected community and official Anthropic skills from submodules.
Sync Agent Kit MCP permissions into global `~/.claude/settings.json`.
You need up-to-date library documentation or framework examples.
A task needs structured reasoning or multi-step planning.
Persistent knowledge should be stored or retrieved across sessions.

items.title: block-dangerous-bash
items.body: Blocks destructive shell patterns such as force push, hard reset, dangerous deletes, database destruction, `kill`, and unsafe permissions.

items.title: check-secrets
items.body: Blocks writes that appear to contain API keys, access tokens, or private keys.

items.title: gitnexus-auto-rebuild
items.body: Runs `npx gitnexus analyze` after writes and edits so the knowledge graph stays fresh.

sections.title: Maintenance
sections.intro: Use these rules when changing Agent Kit itself.

blocks.title: Source of truth
blocks.content: Edit `global/` or `custom/`, not installed files under `~/.claude/`. Do not edit `skills/claudekit-skills/` or `skills/anthropics-skills/` because they are Git submodules.

python3 tests/run_all.py
python3 tests/test_kit.py
python3 tests/test_init_project_script.py
python3 custom/hooks/tests/test_hooks.py

sections.title: Reader Checklist
sections.intro: Use this section to orient quickly before making changes or installing assets.

items.title: If you are installing Agent Kit
items.body: Run `python3 scripts/install.py`, initialize submodules, and check status before using commands in target projects.

items.title: If you are modifying Agent Kit
items.body: Update source files, sync docs, regenerate visual documentation when needed, and run `python3 tests/run_all.py`.

items.title: If you are using it in another project
items.body: Run `/ak:init-project` first, then add skills and custom assets only after reviewing what will be installed.

items.title: If docs look stale
items.body: Trust source files over generated docs, then run `/ak:sync-docs` and regenerate HTML from `docs/raw/`.
